title: Email → Governed Project Data
subtitle: Demo 1 · inbound email, integrated to Forma

inputs.label: Incoming email
inputs.description: A vague email with a generically-named attachment lands in the shared project inbox.
inputs.inputFormat: An email with a free-text body and a PDF/CAD attachment.

persona.quote: Every attachment arrives as 'final_v3.pdf' — I rename and re-file dozens by hand every day.

stations.label: CAPTURE
stations.description: Ingests and validates the incoming item.

agents.name: Review
agents.description: Validates the source and confirms a live project.
agents.output: A verified item bound to the right project.

stations.label: UNDERSTAND
stations.description: Reads the content and classifies it.

agents.name: Vision
agents.description: Reads the attachment, including scanned pages.
agents.output: Extracted text and detected document type.

agents.name: Classification
agents.output: A tagged item: discipline, location, type.

stations.label: STRUCTURE
stations.description: Creates a structured record with a compliant name.

agents.name: Ticketing
agents.description: Creates a typed record from the content.

stations.label: ASSURE
stations.description: Runs the final compliance checks before anything is written.

agents.name: Data QA
agents.description: Confirms all required fields are present.
agents.output: A completeness pass / fail with reasons.

agents.name: Compliance
agents.description: Verifies the name conforms to the standard.
agents.output: A CONFORMING record, ready to write to Forma.

outputs.label: Typed Issue
outputs.description: A correctly-typed issue created in Autodesk Forma.

outputs.label: Filed Document
outputs.description: The attachment, archived under a compliant name and linked to its issue.

steps.caption: A document controller is drowning in manual renaming and filing.
An email arrives — vague body, generic attachment name.
The Review Agent ingests and validates the item.
The Classification Agent reads the content: discipline, location, type.
The Ticketing and Naming Agents build a structured, compliantly-named record.
The Compliance Agent verifies naming and required fields.
A governed, conforming Issue is created in Forma.
And the attachment is filed as a compliant, linked document.
